rules.description: Use Read tool instead of bash cat/head/tail for file reading

action.alternative: Only use Bash for streaming/real-time scenarios (tail -f, live logs)

rules.rationale: Read tool optimized for file viewing, better UX, handles large files, shows line numbers

examples.scenario: User: 'Show me the contents of config.json'
examples.correct: Use Read tool to read config.json

rules.description: Use Grep tool instead of bash grep/rg for code search

action.alternative: Only use Bash grep for complex pipelines

rules.rationale: Grep tool optimized for search, supports output modes, better performance

rules.description: Use Glob tool instead of bash find/ls for file discovery

action.alternative: Only use Bash find for complex predicates (mtime, size, etc.)

rules.rationale: Glob tool optimized for pattern matching, faster, cleaner results

rules.description: Use Edit tool instead of bash sed/awk for file editing

action.alternative: Only use Bash sed/awk for streaming scenarios

rules.rationale: Edit tool ensures exact matching, prevents errors, preserves formatting

rules.description: Use Write tool instead of bash echo/cat for file creation
rules.rationale: Write tool safer, handles special characters, clear intent

examples.correct: Use Write tool with file_path and content

rules.description: Never use Bash echo to communicate with user
rules.rationale: Bash is for system operations, not communication. Direct output clearer.

examples.scenario: AI wants to explain next steps

rules.description: Use Task tool with Explore subagent for non-needle codebase exploration

action.alternative: Direct Glob/Grep only for needle queries (specific class/function)

rules.rationale: Explore agent optimized for broad codebase understanding, reduces token usage

examples.scenario: User: 'Where are errors from the client handled?'
examples.correct: Use Task tool with Explore subagent
examples.incorrect: Use Grep directly to search for 'error' everywhere

examples.correct: Use Grep to find 'class ErrorHandler' (needle query)
examples.incorrect: Use Task tool for simple class search

rules.description: Make independent tool calls in parallel within single message

action.alternative: Sequential calls only when dependencies exist

rules.rationale: Maximize performance, reduce latency, follow Claude Code best practices

examples.correct: Single message with 3 Read tool calls
examples.incorrect: 3 separate messages with 1 Read call each

examples.scenario: Read file A, then use contents to search file B
examples.correct: Sequential: Read A, then search B with results
examples.incorrect: Parallel calls (would fail, B search needs A contents)
